title: How To Improve Your CTR On Thumbnails

transcript.text: If your click-through rate is 4%, that means [music] that 96 out of 100 people saw your video and chose not to watch it. Why did 96% of the people choose not to click in the first place? Well, in Carol's case, her old titles were accurate [music] and unfortunately completely unclickable. They kind of lacked tension. And that's a mistake a lot of creators make. They try to be clear, but they fail at being compelling. So, Carol's coaching shifted into interrogative [music] packaging. And look at these breakouts. Seven red flags that undermined his entire story. Was he telling the truth? This testimony destroyed his defense. These titles create a curiosity [music] gap, the distance between what the thumbnail shows and what the viewer doesn't know yet. Notice how these videos aren't news, but they're [music] an interrogation of the news. But you have to be careful about this because the difference between creating a curiosity gap and click-baiting someone can be a fine line.
